trivia_bullets.text: Runesword was first printed in The Dark (August 1994), set in the aftermath of the Brothers' War Sylex Blast: a world of cold, shadow, and anti-magic religious persecution. This gothic medieval-European aesthetic defined the card's dark-fantasy tone.
sources: MTG Wiki: The Dark, via WebSearch

trivia_bullets.text: The Chronicles (July 1995) copy in the inventory is from Magic's first reprint compilation set. Chronicles reprinted cards from Arabian Nights, Antiquities, Legends, and The Dark; its market impact was so severe it directly prompted Wizards to create the Reserved List. Runesword was uncommon in The Dark but was downgraded to common in Chronicles.

Illustrated by Christopher Rush (1965–2016), one of the original 25 MTG artists. Rush painted Black Lotus and also helped design the five mana-color symbols. He worked on Magic from Alpha through several early sets.

Approximately one-third of Runesword copies from The Dark carry a documented misprint: an uneven black line across the text box resembling a hair caught in the printing press. Scryfall catalogues this variant separately as card 107† (daggered). The Chronicles reprint does not carry this defect.
sources: WebSearch: eBay misprint listings; Scryfall DRK 107†

related_cards.name: Runesword
related_cards.note: Original printing (uncommon); Christopher Rush art; site of the famous 'hairy' misprint variant
